---
title: "Select orthologues of arabidopsis clock genes in maize"
format: html
date: "2026-02-12"
---

1. *Arabidopsis thaliana* proteins with "rhythmic process" (GO:0048511) GO annotation were retrieved from UniProt Release 2026_01.

```{bash gene-list}
# extract arabidopsis UPs
zcat uniprotkb_organism_id_3702_AND_go_00485_2026_02_13.tsv.gz | cut -f1 | tail -n +2 > at_UP

# UPs and description
zcat uniprotkb_organism_id_3702_AND_go_00485_2026_02_13.tsv.gz | cut -f1,6 -d $'\t' | tail -n +2 | sort -k1,1 -t $'\t' > at_UP_descr

# extract arabidopsis ensembl gene ids
zcat uniprotkb_organism_id_3702_AND_go_00485_2026_02_13.tsv.gz | cut -f2 -d $'\t' | tr ';' '\n' | cut -f1 -d"." | sort -u | cat -n | tail -n +2 | head -n -1 > at_gramene
```

2. Maize orthologues were retrieved with BioMart from gramene (Ensembl Plants Genes 62).
  **Method 1**: retrieve using UPs and "UniProtKB Gene Name IDs", "UniProtKB/SwissProt" and "UniProtKB/TrEMBL"

```{bash check m1}
# count unique rows
awk -F'\t' '$2!=""' mart-up-genename | sort -u | wc -l
# 217
awk -F'\t' '$2!=""' mart-up-swiss | sort -u | wc -l
# 215
awk -F'\t' '$2!=""' mart-up-trembl | sort -u | wc -l
# 28

# concatenate and clean
# total number of orthologues
cat mart-up* | awk -F'\t' '$2!=""' | sort -u | wc -l
# 220
cat mart-up* | awk -F'\t' '$2!=""' | sort -u > mart-all-up

# unique At UPs
sort -k1,1 -t $'\t' -u mart-all-up | wc -l
# 86
# unique NAMs (maize genes)
sort -k2,2 -t $'\t' -u mart-all-up | wc -l
# 193
# extensive duplication
# Conclusion - filtering is needed
```

  **Method 2**: retrieve using gramene IDs
```{bash check m2}
# count unique rows
awk -F'\t' '$2!=""' mart-gramene | sort -u | wc -l
# 215
awk -F'\t' '$2!=""' mart-gramene | sort -u > mart-all-gramene
```

```{bash compare}
# compare
diff mart-all-gramene mart-all-up
# 41a42
# > AT2G21070     Zm00001eb208320 0       56.4103 51.462
# 56a58
# > AT3G01060     Zm00001eb292620 0       76.082  73.4066
# 87a90,91
# > AT3G55960     Zm00001eb148930 1       46.978  56.0656
# > AT3G55960     Zm00001eb368330 1       54.3408 55.4098
# 115a120
# > AT4G02630     Zm00001eb348580 1       57.9158 58.7398

# so using UP IDs gives 5 more orthologues
```

3. Add protein names

Also add zea UPs using file [00-at-ortho.qmd](../../../input-data/at-ortho_rhythmic-process/00-at-ortho.qmd)
```{bash add names}
# Split 2nd field by ; to have one gramene ID per line

awk -F'\t' -v OFS='\t' '
{
    n = split($2, arr, /;/)   # podziel $2 po ";", wynik w tablicy arr[1..n]

    # Dla każdego elementu wypisz cały wiersz, ale z jedną wartością w kolumnie 2
    for (i = 1; i <= n; i++) {
        $2 = arr[i];
        print
    }
}
' < x > x2

# remove lines without ID in 2nd field, remove version number and select unique rows
awk -F'\t' -v OFS='\t' '$2!=""' x2 | sed 's/\./\t/' | cut -f3 -d $'\t' --complement | sort -u | sort -k2,2 -t $'\t' > UP_gramene_names

# add names to orthologue list
join -1 1 -2 2 -t $'\t' mart-all-gramene UP_gramene_names  > x
mv x mart-all-gramene

join -1 1 -2 2 -t $'\t' mart-all-up UP_gramene_names  > x
wc -l x
# 214 x

# so not all IDs have names, why?
join -1 1 -2 2 -t $'\t' -a1 -o auto mart-all-up UP_gramene_names  > x
awk -F'\t' '$7==""' x
# AT2G21070       Zm00001eb208320 0       56.4103 51.462
# AT3G01060       Zm00001eb292620 0       76.082  73.4066
# AT3G55960       Zm00001eb148930 1       46.978  56.0656
# AT3G55960       Zm00001eb368330 1       54.3408 55.4098
# AT4G02630       Zm00001eb348580 1       57.9158 58.7398

# manual check in UniProt and gramene showed that these 4 At genes are not port of central clock so can be ignored

# Conclusion - orthologue mapping with gramene IDs will be used further

# Differentially expressed genes in each project and tissue can be selected from mart-all-gramene file.

# Add maize UPs to mart-all-gramene

sort -k2,2 -t $'\t' mart-all-gramene > x
mv x mart-all-gramene

join -1 1 -2 2 -t $'\t' ../go-uniprot/NAM2UP_all_forGOextraction mart-all-gramene > mart-all-gramene_up-zea
```
